title: Koehneago caripensis
intro: Koehneago caripensis, also known as Guaco or Mexican Pennywort, is a perennial plant native to Central America, characterized by its small, white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Koehneago caripensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koehneago caripensis is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Koehneago caripensis is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification places it among the monocots, a group of flowering plants that have a single cotyledon in their seeds. Within the Poaceae family, Koehneago caripensis is a species of grass that is native to specific regions. Further classification within the Poaceae family would involve the subfamily, tribe, and genus levels, but this information is not readily available. The classification of Koehneago caripensis is an area of ongoing research and study.
